methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Radian Forge is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jeff Yeager that has raised $3M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private robotics firms and one public comparator, though narrow peer availability constrains the precision of multiples-based approaches.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable technique, given data limitations. Using the $3M total raised as a proxy for seed valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier—standard for Seed to next-stage progression in this sector—yields an implied valuation of $8M. A peer-median approach was not viable; while 12 same-sector companies exist, filtering for stage, valuation tier, and capital proximity yielded insufficient datapoints (fewer than three matches required). Applying an all-sector median would introduce material noise and was rejected.

The triangulated range is $6M to $9M, with a median of $8M, supported by the single applicable method. The analysis carries meaningful uncertainty around this point estimate given reliance on a single methodological pillar. Key risks to valuation include: absence of comparable seed-stage robotics exits to calibrate step-up multiples, stage-specific market conditions, and execution risk inherent in the seed cohort. Additional clarity on Radian Forge's capital efficiency, technical differentiation, and path to Series A would refine confidence in this range.
